title: Coelogyne teleensis
taxonomy: Coelogyne teleensis is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Coelogyne is a part of the subtribe Coelogyninae. Coelogyne teleensis is a terrestrial orchid native to the Himalayas. It is known to thrive in shaded, humid environments at high altitudes.

intro: Coelogyne teleensis, also known as the Teleensis Coelogyne, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Borneo. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as skin infections and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne teleensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
